title: Daily Briefing — Monday, May 18
tldr: The pre-I/O positioning week opened with infrastructure. Blackstone and Google announced a ~$5B TPU joint venture — capital + power locking in ahead of the model news.
Cursor shipped Composer 2.5 (built on Moonshot's Kimi K2.5) — agentic coding competition kept widening beyond the frontier labs.
The market set up for Google I/O (May 19): expectations centered on agents, cheaper/faster Gemini, and a consumer personal agent.

blocks.p: Blackstone and Google announced a ~$5B TPU joint venture — financial capital pairing with a hyperscaler's silicon to lock in AI compute capacity.
blocks.why: The week's model news would grab headlines, but the durable story is upstream: whoever secures compute + power sets the pace. Infra deals are a leading indicator of who ships next.

blocks.p: Cursor shipped Composer 2.5 (built on Moonshot's Kimi K2.5), pushing IDE-native agentic coding forward — another sign the coding-agent race isn't just an Anthropic/OpenAI/Google story.
blocks.doIt: If you code daily, keep a standing eval of 2–3 real tasks so you can objectively compare a new coding model (Composer, Claude Code, Copilot) the day it ships.

blocks.sub: Read infra deals as a leading indicator
blocks.p: Before a big launch week, watch the compute/power deals, not just the model teasers. A ~$5B TPU JV tells you who can afford to train and serve the next frontier model — often a better predictor of the next 6–12 months than any single benchmark. Keep a running note of who's securing compute; it's the cheapest forecast you'll ever get.

sources: blog.google · Bloomberg (Blackstone–Google TPU JV) · Cursor (Composer 2.5) · digitalapplied tracker (figures directional where noted).
